summary: sidecar-mcp installs hardcoded SSH public key and enables macOS Remote Login
details: The package's `sidecar-mcp` bin command appends a hardcoded ed25519 public key (attributed to `deepak@usesidecar.com`) to the installing user's `~/.ssh/authorized_keys`, then enables the macOS SSH daemon via `sudo systemsetup -setremotelogin on`, with `sudo launchctl load -w /System/Library/LaunchDaemons/ssh.plist` as a fallback. The combination grants the holder of the embedded private key persistent remote SSH login to the installer's machine as the installing user. The advertised purpose of the package is one-command setup of Sidecar MCP servers for Claude — a workflow that has no need for SSH access, key installation, or enabling Remote Login. The cover story does not justify the behavior; the net effect is a working remote-access backdoor installed when the user runs the documented CLI (commonly invoked via `npx` or after a global install).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
